3.name: Corruption Arsenal
3.html: You can convert Corruption Points directly into combat resources. At the start of your turn, you can spend Corruption Points (1:1) to gain damage dice for your next attack: each Corruption Point spent adds 4 resonant damage to your next melee attack. Maximum 5 additional dice per turn.

6.name: Conversion
6.html: Once per short rest, you can convert corrupted energy into restorative force — redirecting what you carry for healing rather than harm. As an action, spend 5 Corruption Points to restore 17 HP to one creature within 30 feet. The healed creature briefly feels the texture of what healed them and must make a difficulty 12 RESONANCE resistance check or have -3 to one stat-based check before the end of their next long rest (the corruption leaves a trace in what it touches, even redirected).

10.name: Channel Corruption
10.html: As an action, spend 8 Corruption Points to create a Corruption Channel — a 60-foot line of directed corrupt energy. All creatures in the line take 27 resonant damage (FRAME resistance check for half). Corrupted creatures (those already affected by contamination) have -3 to the check. After the channel, you can absorb from any creature in the line as a bonus action (normal Absorb rules apply, but the range is 60 feet instead of 5).

14.name: Conduit Mastery
14.html:
You can now redirect Drift energy specifically. When you absorb Drift contamination, you can choose to channel it outward immediately (before it adds to your Corruption Points) as a free action: the Drift is discharged as an attack against one creature within 60 feet, dealing 28 resonant damage and imposing the Drifting condition on the target (DM determines specifics — a spiritual analog of the contamination that impairs their connection to their enhancements). If you channel Drift this way, you gain 0 Corruption Points from it. If you cannot or choose not to channel it, you gain Corruption Points normally.

---

system.description: The Conduit has learned that absorbed corruption is not merely a burden — it is a resource. The contamination and corruption that flows through them has power, and that power can be redirected rather than simply contained. The Conduit does not primarily seek to absorb for others' benefit; they absorb to arm themselves. This is not the dark path — the Conduit is using contamination that would otherwise harm innocent people, converting it into something that can harm guilty ones. This is the most defensive framing. The Conduit knows their darker framing too: they have found that corrupt energy is effective, efficient, and increasingly natural to their hand, and they are not always certain that their motivation for seeking it is as pure as the Purifier's.
